mesa 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • Development Log
    • Getting Started
  • Changelog

Package index

Terms: the atoms

Individual variables carrying their causal identity — a role, a label, levels, and the other attributes that make a term more than a name.

tm() is_tm() experimental
Create vectorized terms
update(<tm>)
Update tm objects
describe()
Describe attributes of a tm vector
set_data() experimental
Stamp data-derived attributes onto terms
filter(<tm>)
Extending dplyr for tm class

Formulas: composition

Terms join and merge into families of related formulas, expanded by patterns and played with through fluent verbs.

fmls() is_fmls() key_terms()
Vectorized formulas
add_strata() remove_strata() add_terms() remove_terms() swap_outcome() subset_data() experimental
Fluent verbs for playing with formula families
apply_pattern() apply_fundamental_pattern() apply_direct_pattern() apply_sequential_pattern() apply_parallel_pattern()
Apply patterns to formulas
register_pattern() experimental
Register a formula expansion pattern
formula_patterns() term_roles() term_transformations()
The mesa vocabulary

Models: formulas meet data

A plan is drawn up (formula by stratum by subset), executed against a fitting approach, and wrapped with its causal context intact.

fit(<fmls>) experimental
Fit the family of models a fmls object describes
fit_plan() experimental
Draw up the fitting plan for a family of formulas
mdl() model() experimental
Model Prototypes
reexports fit tidy glance
Objects exported from other packages

Collections: the notebook of models

Many models stored, recalled, and compared in one table.

print(<mdl_tbl>) summary(<mdl_tbl>) model_table() mdl_tbl() is_model_table() experimental
Model tables
attach_data() model_failures() term_table() formula_matrix() model_data() experimental
Model table helper functions
flatten_models() experimental
Flatten a model table to its parameter estimates
estimate_interaction() experimental
Estimating interaction effect estimates

Tables: the mesa

Laying families of models out for papers, built on the gt package.

tbls
Extensions of gt package for multiple models
tbl_beta()
Table of linear and generalized models
tbl_dichotomous_hazard() tbl_categorical_hazard()
Table of hazard ratios
tbl_interaction_forest()
Forest plot and table
theme_gt_compact()
Compact and minimal theme for gt tables

Helpers

lhs() rhs()
Tools for working with formula-like objects
labeled_formulas_to_named_list()
Convert labeling formulas to named lists
number_of_missing() is_dichotomous()
Data summarization and classification methods

On this page

Developed by Anish S. Shah.
